data.title: Colombia's peso underperforms LatAm peers on leftist lead in presidential polls
data.description:
Key points:
  • MSCI LatAm stocks down 0.4%, FX up 0.4%
  • Oil holds above $100 a barrel as U.S.-Iran talks stall
  • Central bank decisions in Brazil and Chile
  • Electoral polls in Colombia, Peru, Brazil in focus

By Johann M Cherian and Purvi Agarwal

Colombia's peso slid 2.3% on Monday after presidential race polls showed left-wing candidate Ivan Cepeda in the lead, tanking investor sentiment about the outlook for the Latin American country that has struggled to balance its public accounts.

Cepeda's lead would suggest policy continuity from incumbent President Gustavo Petro, who has pursued reforms that ballooned the fiscal deficit and government debt, triggering downgrades into junk status by credit rating agencies Fitch and S&P Global within the past six months.

The peso marked its biggest one-day drop in over a year and stocks lost 1.2%, while hard-currency bonds (US195325EM30=TE), (US195325BK01=TE) were marginally higher.

State-run energy company Ecopetrol fell 2.8% as higher crude prices and strong earnings did little to stymie the uncertainties around elections.

Brazil is also headed for elections between October and November and a BTG Pactual/Nexus poll showed that incumbent President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva and Senator Flavio Bolsonaro are statistically tied in a simulated runoff for this year's presidential election.

Brazil's public debt dropped in March from the previous month as net bond redemptions offset an increase in interest payments, Treasury data showed.

The real was flat ahead of an interest rate decision on Wednesday, while stocks slipped 0.6%, cushioned by Petrobras' 0.3% rise.

"The election outcome in both countries is crucial to the outlook for asset prices in the next few years in each," Gillian Edgworth, fixed income portfolio manager, emerging markets, Wellington Management, said on the outlook for Brazil and Colombia.

"Both countries have significant fiscal challenges. Both countries are going into these elections with large budget deficits and ... with interest rates that are high. "

Election jitters were also prevalent in Peru as a poll showed right-wing candidate Keiko Fujimori is heading for a dead-heat presidential runoff against leftist Roberto Sanchez as the vote count drags on. The runoff is expected in June.

The sol weakened 0.6% to hit its lowest since September, while MSCI's benchmark for local stocks (.MIPE00000PUS) shed 0.9%.

More broadly, MSCI's index for stocks (.MILA00000PUS) dipped 0.4%, while a gauge for currencies (.MILA00000CUS) firmed 0.4% as talks for a peace deal between Iran and the U.S. remained stalled.

Crude prices hit a two-week high at $108 a barrel as shipments through the Strait of Hormuz were thin. Analysts say higher-for-longer prices could weigh on global economic growth including that of Latin America that has fared relatively better among emerging markets.

"Resilience is one reason why we are becoming more constructive on EM FX. Positioning was light going into the shock, the rebound after the ceasefire was strong, and real-rate differentials remain compelling in selected markets," said Nicolas Jullien, global head of fixed income at Candriam.

"We continue to favor Latin America, particularly Brazil and Mexico, where carry and, in particular carry-to-volatility, remains compelling and external dynamics are still supportive."

data.title: RMBS: Q1 2026 delivered robust revenue growth and cash flow, with strong outlook for AI-driven products
data.description:

Original document: Rambus, Inc. [RMBS] SEC 8-K Current Report — Apr. 27 2026

data.title: Barfresh Food Group Inc expected to post a loss of 8 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Barfresh Food Group Inc is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 29 (estimated) for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Los Angeles California-based company is expected to report a 73.2% increase in revenue to $5.076 million from $2.93 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data. (Basis is on NET/TOTAL REVENUE)

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Barfresh Food Group Inc is for a loss of 8 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 128.6% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Barfresh Food Group Inc is $5.25, about 96.6% above its last closing price of $2.67

data.title: CINF: Q1 2026 net income surged to $274M, with improved underwriting and lower catastrophe losses
data.description:
Net income rebounded to $274 million in Q1 2026 from a $90 million loss in Q1 2025, driven by higher premiums, investment income, and sharply lower catastrophe losses. The combined ratio improved to 95.6%, and the company continued its long streak of dividend increases.

data.title: Solaris Energy beats Q1 revenue, adjusted EPS estimates, raises outlook
data.description:
Overview

  • US power infrastructure provider's Q1 revenue rose, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 beat analyst expectations

  • Company raised Q2 adjusted EBITDA guidance and signed new 10-year power contract

Outlook

  • Company raises Q2 2026 Adjusted EBITDA guidance to $83-93 mln from $76-84 mln

  • Solaris sets Q3 2026 Adjusted EBITDA guidance at $80-95 mln

  • Company expects deployments for new 10-year power contract to begin in late 2026

Result Drivers

  • POWER SOLUTIONS GROWTH - Revenue in the Power Solutions segment rose 24% sequentially, driven by a 17% increase in average capacity earning revenue

  • NEW LONG-TERM CONTRACT - Co signed a 10-year agreement to provide over 600 MW of power capacity to a global technology customer, with deployments expected to begin in late 2026

  • LOGISTICS SEGMENT DECLINE - Logistics Solutions revenue fell 11% sequentially due to lower last-mile transportation activity

data.description:
• National Fuel Gas Co , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 29 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Williamsville New York-based company is expected to report a 16.6% increase in revenue to $851.483 million from $729.95 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 5 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​
